# Tomato Mien

> A free productivity reminder and Pomodoro timer app for macOS, Windows, and Linux.

Tomato Mien is a free, open-source, cross-platform desktop productivity application. It helps users stay on track with smart reminder rules, interval timers, and Pomodoro focus sessions — all running fully offline with native desktop notifications.

## What It Does

Tomato Mien is a **productivity tool** and **reminder app** that lets you:

- Set work break reminders on a repeating interval (e.g. every 25 or 30 minutes)
- Schedule specific-time alarms (e.g. a meeting reminder at 2:25 PM)
- Restrict when reminders fire using time range filters (e.g. only during 9 AM – 6 PM)
- Run a **Pomodoro timer** with 25-minute focus sessions and 5-minute breaks
- Manage multiple reminder rules from a searchable dashboard
- Receive native desktop notifications with sound alerts

## Use Cases

- **Focus timer**: Break reminders every 25 minutes during work hours (Pomodoro technique)
- **Hydration reminder**: Drink water every 30 minutes from 9 AM to 6 PM
- **Meeting prep**: Alert 5 minutes before a daily standup at 2:25 PM
- **Hourly stretch**: Stand up and stretch every 60 minutes
- **Medication alert**: Morning and evening reminders at fixed times
- **Night study timer**: Track study sessions with 20-minute intervals

## Key Features

- Productivity reminder and time management for desktop
- Pomodoro timer with configurable focus and break cycles
- Interval reminders (every N minutes)
- Specific time alarms (exact hour and minute)
- Time range filters (active only during work hours)
- Rule-based scheduling: combine multiple triggers per rule
- Background operation via Web Worker — fires even when minimized
- Native desktop notifications with sound
- Fully offline — no internet connection required
- Free and open-source (MIT License)
- Cross-platform: macOS, Windows, Linux

## Platforms

- macOS (.dmg) — Apple Silicon and Intel
- Windows (.exe)
- Linux (.AppImage)

## Category

Productivity, Time Management, Reminder App, Pomodoro Timer, Focus Tool, Desktop App

## How to Use

1. Create a Rule — name your reminder
2. Add Triggers — interval (e.g. every 25 min) or specific time (e.g. 2:30 PM)
3. Set Filters (Optional) — restrict to time ranges like 9 AM – 5 PM
4. Enable & Go — reminders run in the background

## FAQ

- Free? Yes, fully free and open-source under the MIT License.
- Offline? Yes, fully offline. All data stored locally.
- Pomodoro? Enable from Settings > Plugins, then restart the app.
- Background? Yes, a Web Worker evaluates reminders even when minimized.
- Notifications? Native desktop notifications with sound, toggleable per rule.
